issue_title: ExaBGP 3.4.13 announces withdrawn routes after reload
Exabgp sends messages with multiple MP_(UN)REACH_NLRI attributes
issue_body:
When using flowspec announcements or withdraws we have noticed that when sending ipv4 and ipv6 flowspec rules exabgp will group both families into a single update message by using two MP_REACH or MP_UNREACH path attributes one for each family.

According to Section 5 of RFC4271 this is invalid
> The same attribute (attribute with the same type) cannot appear more
> than once within the Path Attributes field of a particular UPDATE
> message.

When testing this on a Juniper MX router it sends us a notification message back about malformed attribute list. The cisco ASR9k we tested just seems to ignore the second MP_REACH attribute.

[exabgp-malformed-batched-mpreach-nlris-example.pcap.gz](https://github.com/Exa-Networks/exabgp/files/571873/exabgp-malformed-batched-mpreach-nlris-example.pcap.gz)

https://docker-py.readthedocs.org need to be updated.

I found mentions of ```cpu_qouta```, ```cpu_period``` options in the changelog but they are missing in the real documentation.

issue_title: Cannot pickle objects when using unix socket
issue_body:
One of my use-cases is loading a list of containers in one process and then pushing them to be processed in another process, using a standard python queue.

While this method works well with the `tcp://` socket, when using the `unix://` socket the pickler will throw the following exception:
```
    queue.put((QUEUE_OBJ, obj), block=block)
  File "<string>", line 2, in put
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/multiprocessing/managers.py", line 756, in _callmethod
    conn.send((self._id, methodname, args, kwds))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/multiprocessing/connection.py", line 206, in send
    self._send_bytes(_ForkingPickler.dumps(obj))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/multiprocessing/reduction.py", line 51, in dumps
    cls(buf, protocol).dump(obj)
AttributeError: Can't pickle local object 'UnixAdapter.__init__.<locals>.<lambda>'
```

On one hand I could just push the IDs of the objects downstream, but I need some of the attributes belonging to the Containers as well, and it makes for quite an inconvenient way of sharing them.

I am not sure if that is the expected behaviour; but the TCP protocol works well enough and I can't see a reason why UNIX should not.

It would be great to have a module `docker.machine` already integrated into this project once [docker-machine](https://github.com/docker/machine) hits 1.0 - machine is turning into a really fantastic project and their dev team is quite active. If you ask around on Freenode (#docker-machine) and look at their [Roadmap](https://github.com/docker/machine/blob/master/ROADMAP.md) you'll find that their plans include a client/server setup with a REST api that external libraries can use to interact with `docker-machine`, very similar to docker itself. That will be a while coming, as they are currently working towards [0.3.0](https://github.com/docker/machine/issues?q=is%3Aopen+is%3Aissue+milestone%3A0.3.0) milestone which will not address this client/server+REST setup. 

I have written some initial code to use `docker-machine` from Python. It's not optimal - it wraps the binary and uses subprocess. However, it works, and it allows me to use docker-machine from setups like Heroku by automatically downloading the docker-machine binary, creating a machine based off my environment variables, and connecting docker-py's client to the created machine. I'm well aware it's not optimal to wrap the binary (or to download random binaries from the internet....), but the code does work in a beta capacity. 

Question is - would docker-py be interested in a PR that contains my code to start test-driving beta support for docker-machine? My thought is that this could trigger other PRs to develop a more full-featured API for machine, with the internal codebase being switched to the server/client REST interface once it's available. For example, their current codebase is heavily centered around concept of Drivers and the Engine, so this would allow docker-py to start accepting PRs that have classes which represent these constructs. As their API becomes more finalized over time, these classes could also solidify 

[Here](https://github.com/hamiltont/WebJuice/blob/master/webjuice/utils.py#L52) is code to automate downloading docker-machine. 
[Here](https://github.com/hamiltont/WebJuice/blob/master/webjuice/utils.py#L102) is code to read environment variables and create a machine based off those variables. Pretty simple - any environment variable prefixed with `DM_` will be converted into an argument flag that's passed to `docker-machine create` e.g. `DM_DRIVER=digitalocean` translates into `docker-machine create --driver digitalocean`
[Here](https://github.com/hamiltont/WebJuice/blob/master/webjuice/utils.py#L173) is code that sets up `docker.Client` using the standard `kwargs_from_env`

>>> graph = nx.complete_graph()
>>> graph.add_node(1)
>>> dnx.treewidth_branch_and_bound(graph)

`ValueError: min() arg is an empty sequence`

CPD headers are incorrectly displayed in ipython by _repr_html (and not displayed at all in with print) when there is more than one evidence variable.
Ex:
```python 
cpd_table = pgmpy.factors.TabularCPD('grade', 2,
                       [[0.7, 0.6, 0.4, 0.2],[0.3, 0.4, 0.6, 0.8]], 
                       ['intel', 'diff'], [2, 2])
cpd_table
```
Displays:
TabularCPD for grade
diff		diff_0			diff_1
intel		intel_0	intel_1	intel_0	intel_1
grade_0	0.7000	0.6000	0.4000	0.2000
grade_1	0.3000	0.4000	0.6000	0.8000

but `cpd_table.variables` returns ['grade', 'intel', 'diff'] and `cpd[1,0,1]` returns 0.4 rather than 0.6 as indicated by the display.
Removing the list reversal in _repr_html gives the proper column labels, but I haven't tested it for higher dimensional CPDs.

This list reversal was commented out in 2c816fb307a95908facd11b62e26f7b745c86415, then reverted in 2d53e30b8a0f9f2adcd064453929105e91c6ce6b so it seems someone is aware of this, but I'm quite sure ended up on the wrong solution.

As a side note, it would be better to change the marginalization examples in the docstrings to the inputs I used here since otherwise you won't catch this bug due to the symmetry of marginalizing over diff or intel.

issue_title: Verify 32-bit Python 2 is handling ViInt64 correctly
issue_body:
On Python 2, int's size is equals to machine's word size. There's a different type long which can be of any size.
On Python 3, there's no long and int can be of any size.

Our APIs have ViInt64 which is 64-bits. We need to make sure that we are handling values larger than 32-bit correctly in Python 2 32-bit (where word size is 32-bit).

I'm not certain what the best way of doing this is. Possibly we need to inspect Python version and machine bitness and then have class ViInt64 inherit from long rather than int in the Python2 32-bit case.

issue_title: Need a new size mechanism for NI-SCOPE and NI-DCPower
issue_body:
This will allow us to run arbitrary python code defined in metadata such as calling a function. This is because some functions require the size to be calculated in ways that don't fit into the current set of mechanisms and are very specific.

The proposal is to have a mechanism called "python-code".

issue_title: Complain if you explicitly set the "other" framework
issue_body:
Currently, if you call ``.use_twisted()`` and then later call ``.use_asyncio()`` txaio will merrily swap out for the asyncio methods.

It should be an error if you explicitly call ``.use_twisted()`` and ``.use_asyncio()`` in the same process (but not, e.g., if Twisted was auto-selected but you call .use_asyncio() explicitly nor if you call .use_asyncio() > 1 time).

See also https://github.com/crossbario/autobahn-python/issues/547

issue_title: The demo database is not aware of new model versions
issue_body: If you have a query which is in present in the database, it's going to return old model results rather than run the new model. We should add a column to the database which records the model version it used and bust the cache if it doesn't match the current model version.

issue_title: create RealFree in the memory collector
issue_body:
Hi,

I would like to see if it has sense to create the metric RealFree as proposed by @Henner in https://github.com/BrightcoveOS/Diamond/issues/353

In our team we setup a Diamond-Graphite-Seyren pipeline, and we need the MemFree error alert, but it is not reliable right now.

I am happy to submit a Merge Request if you find it interesting

In the freezeThaw [documentation](http://web.mit.edu/music21/doc/moduleReference/moduleFreezeThaw.html#music21.freezeThaw.JSONFreezeThawBase):

```
>>> sf2 = freezeThaw.StreamFreezer(c) # do not reuse StreamFreezers
>>> data2 = sf2.writeStr(fmt='jsonpickle')
>>> st2 = freezeThaw.StreamThawer()
>>> st2.openStr(data2)
>>> s3 = st.stream
>>> len(s3.parts[0].measure(7).notes) == 6
True
```

it seems the s3 line should read:

`>>> s3 = st2.stream`

I would like to see errorbars on the ensemble mean square displacement plot. There are a number of subtleties that could be taken into account. (For example, the coordinates that Trackpy assigns to each particle will have some error. For another example, the single particle MSD error must be calculated with the awareness that the data being averaged are not independent.) 

The easiest way to do with for an ensemble MSD is to calculate the standard deviation when taking the average of the single-particle trajectories.

I think that calculating and plotting errorbars should be built in, if not the default setting.

issue_title: builder: Bad protocol version identification 'dummy-value' from 10.240.190.22
issue_body:
When reading logs from builder, users may see something like:
```
Bad protocol version identification 'dummy-value' from 10.240.190.22
```

This is due to the changes made in #1940 - we had to refactor the ExecStartPost loops to use `ncat` instead of `cat /dev/tcp/...` because of the bash vulnerability fixed in [CoreOS 452.0.0](https://coreos.com/releases/#452.0.0) (specifically, bash no longer has direct network access). We need to pass something to ncat to send over the wire, so we use 'dummy-value'. We don't care that it's an invalid string - the import thing is that ncat can connect.

So, this is innocuous, but ideally we shouldn't need a string at all - maybe we can just send an end of transmission character?

issue_title: Publisher fails to parse HEALTHCHECK_INITIAL_DELAY if the value contains `\r`
issue_body:
When using
```
deis config:push -p staging.env -a my-app
```
if config file being pushed contains Windows style line endings (`\r\n`), then `\r` will be treated as part of the value, which will result in error when trying to parse `HEALTHCHECK_INITIAL_DELAY` from uploaded config:
```
2016/09/03 01:47:43 strconv.ParseInt: parsing "10\r": invalid syntax
```

issue_title: Use explicit scopes instead of defaults for `globus login`
issue_body:
We'd like to be a little bit more stable to potential changes we make in the SDK.
It's not _wrong_ right now, but it would be easy for us to carelessly alter it.
https://github.com/globus/globus-cli/blob/8dbf936a7fb572bea5c7de1bd705bc7d8ca8ee55/globus_cli/commands/login.py#L108-L109

Simple solution: put scope list in a constant, pass it there instead.

* `preserve-mtime`
* `perf-pp`, `perf-cc`, `perf-p`, `perf-udt`
* `verify-checksum` (default: yes)
* `label` (set)

Conversation topics to get us going:

- [x] what additional nose tests need adding to globus_sdk/tests?
- [x] for testing methods that are actually supposed to call live services, do we want to use real test tokens, or do we want to fake the services, or both?
- [ ] expand to AppVeyor testing for Windows? add OS X on Travis?

This should be a simple get call to the successful_transfers resource on the given task.

Docs:
https://docs.globus.org/api/transfer/task/#get_task_successful_transfers

issue_body:
### What is wrong?

Recently, someone on gitter asked if Trinity supports spinning up custom nets without touching the default code base. So, far that doesn't seem to be the case (maybe through subtile hacks idk). There seems to be a great opportunity to break up our existing node logic (full / light, mainnet / ropsten) into more fine grained building blocks to enable plugins to deliver such functionality. E.g. it would be nice if someone could just write a plugin to e.g. support the `Kovan` net (just using `Kovan` as an example, I have zero knowlege about any potential blockers with that).

### How can it be fixed

Lots of exploration.

issue_title: Add a parameter in Community graphs
issue_body:
Community graph has a fixed epsilon-NN graph embedded in the construction of the classes.

We should replace 1e-3 by a parametrized value, potentially even depending on Nc and N.

issue_title: Use REST interface responses with ACE interface
issue_body: The ACE interface currently uses a response format different from the REST one. The REST one is arguably better, so the ACE interface should use it, too. Perhaps optionally, at first, to not break backward compatibility, then by default for v0.6.0 (with notifications to people who use the current form).

issue_title: Consider leaving root env on PATH with activate
issue_body:
Advantages:
- This would be more consistent with unix behavior of how PATH is built up during login with append/prepend exports.  Consider a non-python package--e.g. git.  If I install `conda install git` in my root env because I know conda's git is more up-to-date than my system git, and then activate a conda env, I definitely don't want to fall back to the system `/usr/bin/git` while working in a non-root conda env.
- It's more consistent with virtualenv/rbenv/nodenv behavior.
- It's a first step in the direction of "stacked" envs.

Implications:
- Potential confusion with something like ipython (basically different versions of python) was mentioned as a reason for removing the root env from PATH on activate.  However, with activate, we don't completely overwrite PATH, so users could still encounter confusion with other pythons, say system /usr/bin/python or homebrew /usr/local/bin/pip on OS X.
- symlinking conda/activate/deactivate wouldn't be necessary anymore.  Keeping the root env on PATH would mean that conda and activate are already naturally available for use within a conda env.

CC @pzwang let's discuss in person sometime soon

issue_title: Can't install PIL because of conflict with spectral
issue_body:
>conda install PIL
solving environment: failed

UnsatisfiableError: the following specifications were found to be in conflict:
-pil
-spectral
Use "conda info <package>" to see the dependencies for each package.

@stefanseefeld asked me to submit this issue.  We have discussed this before at length.  Conda-build needs to completely rebuild its index after any package is built, so that that package is then installable for the test phase.  It would be a large optimization to conda-build if conda were able to modify the existing index to add the new package, rather than re-generate the entire index.

Some more info (possibly outdated) here:

This happens in two parts.  The first adds the package to the on-disk repodata.json file: https://github.com/conda/conda-build/blob/master/conda_build/build.py#L755
MichaelS
The second re-reads the on-disk repodata.json into the memory object: https://github.com/conda/conda-build/blob/master/conda_build/build.py#L1239
MichaelS
It would be ideal to replace the second part with a small modification to the in-memory index during the first part.

issue_title: Add control over which packages to export from an environment
issue_body:
This seems to be related to but not a duplicate of #5155.

I like to keep only dependencies that are imported directly in my `environment.yml` file to keep it cross-platform, i.e., let conda find the dependencies of the top-level dependencies automatically. It would be great if I could only export the versions of the dependencies listed there when calling `conda env export`.

Usage example:

```
conda env export -n myenv --from-file environment.yml > environment.yml
```

Conda would read packages listed in `environment.yml`, and use these to filter what's exported.

Another usage example:

```
conda env export -n myenv --packages python matplotlib pandas
```

This command would only export those packages (whether installed by pip or conda).

issue_title: Add documentation on how to bootstrap conda
issue_body: Bootstrapping conda, either on a new architecture or just building some of the packages that conda depends on, is not trivial. It would be useful to have some documentation on how to do it.

Hello,

I was wondering how to control the Ax12A servos synchronously, not asynchronously. I've been using  dxl_io.set_goal_position, but the program does not execute this synchronously. The program calls this line of code and then immediately moves to the next line, without waiting for the servo to get to that position. I was wondering if there is a function that tells the servo to go to a position and does not go to the next line of code until the servo is there. 

Isn't that a better way to control a robot anyway? With the set_goal_position function, how can I tell servo 1 to go to 30 degrees and then only after that make servo 2 go to 120 degrees. Would you have to use a time.sleep() function? There's got to be a better way.

Thank you so much

When running pysam 0.9.0 with Python >= 3.5 `pysam.__init__.py` tries to use `sysconfig.get_config_var()`; however, the module is never imported. Seems to be fixed by simply importing the module.

The psyam/TabProxies.pyx needs a very minor update to the GTFProxy.as.Dict() method.

Currently, the attribute lines are split by "; ". Attributes ending in "; " therefore generate a final empty split product (''). Previously the final split was done on ";" and the final split product was discarded but it looks like this was amended to accommodate attributes which end in ";" rather than "; ".  I'd propose the change below.

Line 498:
< fields = [x.strip() for x in attributes.split("; ")]
\> fields = [x.strip() for x in attributes.strip().split("; ")]

I have created a simplified test case for my issue.
fetch() seems to not pick up on the EOF if it is blocking before the EOF is transmitted.
This causes the program to lock up.
Python 3.5 pysam 11.2.1
```
import pysam, multiprocessing, sys

def p(pipeR):
    c = pysam.AlignmentFile(pipeR.fileno())
    d = pysam.AlignmentFile(sys.stdout, 'w', template=c)
    for r in c.fetch(until_eof=True): d.write(r)
    c.close()
    d.close()

a = pysam.AlignmentFile('singlePair250.bam')
pipeR, pipeW = multiprocessing.Pipe(False)
b = pysam.AlignmentFile(pipeW.fileno(), 'wbu', template=a)

proc = multiprocessing.Process(target=p, args=(pipeR,))
proc.start()

for r in a: b.write(r)
a.close()

I use pileups to get all reads on a certain position. issue_title: Snapshots do not work with AWS ES 5.3
issue_body:
As requested in #639 I open this issue:

AWS ES 5.3 has been released recently and they _now_ claim is compatible with Curator: https://aws.amazon.com/about-aws/whats-new/2017/06/elasticsearch-5-3-now-available-on-amazon-elasticsearch-service/
> Curator Support: Enabled usage of the popular Curator utility to simplify index management tasks.

I have been using Curator 5 API to create a Lambda function (here: https://gist.github.com/cjuroz/d45f4d73e74f068892c5e4f3d1c7fa7c) that will help to automate index cleaning and snapshots management. 

So far, index cleaning operations are working pretty fine *BUT* snapshot creation is failing because AWS guys still didn't expose `/_snapshot/_status` endpoint. I already reported this bug here: https://forums.aws.amazon.com/thread.jspa?threadID=257549.

Note: As a workaround, I am using ElasticSearch API to directly send signed request to ES to do snapshots.

issue_title: RequestsHttpConnection attempts to log undefined values
issue_body:
The perform_request method tries to use the response of session.request when an exception has been raised:

```python
        try:
            response = self.session.request(method, url, data=body, timeout=timeout or self.timeout)
        except requests.exceptions.SSLError as e:
            self.log_request_fail(method, url, response.request.path_url, body, time.time() - start, exception=e)
```

`response` will be undefined if an exception is thrown so the logging fails. 

I have forked and created a branch with a suggested fix and test. 
https://github.com/ekelly30/elasticsearch-py/tree/requesthttpconnection-exception-logging

issue_title: Delete a repository from Azure Container Registry
issue_body:
It is not currently possible to delete an image from a repository in Azure Container Registry, either through the UI or the CLI tool -- other than manually looking at all of the digests for all of the layers of a specific image, and deleting those through the blob filesystem (this is an unreasonable method of deletion, prone to human error).

The registry API supports deleting images (all image layers): https://docs.docker.com/registry/spec/api/#deleting-an-image

`az acr` should support deleting an image from a specific registry, perhaps in the format of:

```bash
az acr repository delete --registry MyRegistry --repo someRepo/someImage
```

issue_body:

from azure.storage import CloudStorageAccount
ACC = CloudStorageAccount(account_name, account_key)

for blob in GEN:

to get list of blobs in container and their metadata, You will have:

None
None
None

blob0 = SRV.get_blob_properties(container_name, blob_name)

In both cases we have azure.storage.blob.models.Blob object so there should be 'metadata' property as in Model.

issue_title: Remove a deprecated Travis CI service integration in favor of GitHub App one
issue_body:
As per https://github.com/orgs/aio-libs/teams/admins/discussions/5/comments/4, @asvetlov it's a task for you.

## Long story short

Drop integration under "Services", keep one under "Apps". Then, go to protected branches and make new integration check required.

## Long story short

I think it's not possible to set `secure_proxy_ssl_header` when deploying with gunicorn.

## Expected behaviour

gunicorn config or environment variable should allow me to set `secure_proxy_ssl_header='X-Forwarded-Proto'` so `request.scheme` is correct.

## Actual behaviour

No apparent way to set it.

## Steps to reproduce

Can give more detail if required.

## Your environment

Heroku, but should be the same with any other.

## Long story short

I cannot seem to get the text or json response from a response.

## Expected behaviour

I would be able to extract the json via response.json()

## Actual behaviour

response.json() gives a None type
response.text() doesn't give anything either

## Steps to reproduce

Run this:

`wordpress_url = "https://kominherstel.nl/wp-json/wp/V2/pages/"



async def fetch(session, url):
    async with session.get(url) as response:
        return response

async def main(wordpress_url):
    async with ClientSession() as session:
        response = await fetch(session, wordpress_url)
        print(await response.json())

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()

loop.run_until_complete(main(wordpress_url))`


In a browser "https://kominherstel.nl/wp-json/wp/V2/pages/" will load.

## Your environment
 aiohttp==2.3.10
OS: Ubuntu 16.04 LTS

Hello, I have conversation regarding issue at aio-libs google group [here](https://groups.google.com/d/msg/aio-libs/HGv0lCMX-40/uQcnVaJdBgAJ) but there brief expanation again in any case:
`aiomysql.sa.create_engine()` method does not allow **timeout** parameter.
`aiomysql.sa.Engine` have no **timeout** attrubute.
`aiomysql.Pool` have no **timeout** attribute.
But all this situation were mentioned in docs.
I've provide pull request with docs updated(just remove this missleading 'timeouts') https://github.com/aio-libs/aiomysql/pull/42 if required.

I see that `aiomysql.connect()` method allow **connect_timeout** parameter and same we can provide to `aiomysql.sa.create_engine()` method without exception. But in any case we cannot get **timeout** attribute later from `sa.Engine` instance.

Also it seems that `sa.create_engine()` method and `aiomysql.Pool` class can receive all parameters that low level `aiomysql.connect()` method can. Maybe it reasonable to note some way regarding this in docs?

issue_title: PyPi releases do not always match repo code
issue_body:
While trying to work out why installing `0.0.17` still triggered the issue outlined in #302, I started looking at other versions and discovered that a few of them don't appear to match the code tagged for that release in this repo.

I've done a rudimentary check on the last 5 versions by doing a very lazy test, just checking if the version of `pymysql` matches between the git tag and the pypi release, here are the results:

[`0.0.15`](https://github.com/aio-libs/aiomysql/blob/v0.0.15/setup.py#L7): match
```sh
$ docker container run -it --rm python pip install aiomysql==0.0.15 | grep -F 'Collecting PyMySQL'                                                                                                                 
Collecting PyMySQL>=0.7.5 (from aiomysql==0.0.15)
```

[`0.0.16`](https://github.com/aio-libs/aiomysql/blob/v0.0.16/setup.py#L7): doesn't match
```sh
$ docker container run -it --rm python pip install aiomysql==0.0.16 | grep -F 'Collecting PyMySQL'
Collecting PyMySQL<0.9,>=0.7.5 (from aiomysql==0.0.16)
```
[`0.0.17`](https://github.com/aio-libs/aiomysql/blob/v0.0.17/setup.py#L7): doesn't match
```sh
$ docker container run -it --rm python pip install aiomysql==0.0.17 | grep -F 'Collecting PyMySQL'
Collecting PyMySQL<=0.9.2,>=0.9 (from aiomysql==0.0.17)
```

[`0.0.18`](https://github.com/aio-libs/aiomysql/blob/v0.0.18/setup.py#L7): no release
```sh
$ docker container run -it --rm python pip install aiomysql==0.0.18 | grep -F 'Collecting PyMySQL'
err: No matching distribution found for aiomysql==0.0.18
```

[`0.0.19`](https://github.com/aio-libs/aiomysql/blob/v0.0.19/setup.py#L7): match
```sh
$ docker container run -it --rm python pip install aiomysql==0.0.19 | grep -F 'Collecting PyMySQL'
Collecting PyMySQL<=0.9.2,>=0.9 (from aiomysql==0.0.19)
```

At best this is awfully confusing because according to the code `0.0.17` was the first release to have the conservative version pinning for `pymysql`, but in fact that's some how made it into the `0.0.16` release (which the code doesn't reflect) but _not_ into `0.0.17`.

It's a little unsettling to find that the versions released in PyPi don't always match the tagged code releases. Is this some sort of mistake or quirk in the shipping process? ­¤śĢ 

Possibly related: https://github.com/aio-libs/aiomysql/issues/318

issue_title: Monitoring events may return None and unclean exit

# Description

I wanted to check something in particular: what happens if you only monitor events and the DOCKER_HOST is set to something unreachable.

# Current Behavior

I receive an invalid event, plus the script waits for nothing and if I interrupt it I get an unclean exit.

# Excepted Behavior

The script should not even start, it should notice that the connection cannot be made. Also it should exit cleanly.

# How To Reproduce

To do the test, I picked the example `events.py` and removed about everything except the loop:

```python
#!/usr/bin/env python3

import asyncio
from aiodocker.docker import Docker
from aiodocker.exceptions import DockerError


async def demo(docker):

    subscriber = docker.events.subscribe()

    while True:
        event = await subscriber.get()
        print(f"event: {event!r}")


if __name__ == '__main__':
    loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()
    docker = Docker()
    try:
        # start a monitoring task.
        event_task = loop.create_task(docker.events.run())
        # do our stuffs.
        loop.run_until_complete(demo(docker))
        # explicitly stop monitoring.
        event_task.cancel()
    finally:
        loop.run_until_complete(docker.close())
        loop.close()
```

issue_title: New location for cmd_ln_print_values_r(ps->config, err_get_logfp(), ps_args()) can cause exception
Single Connection Refused Hangs Entire ucsmsdk
issue_body:
Under specific circumstances, the ucsmsdk will hang. The circumstances are as follows:

1) Create a handle to something other than a fully functional UCS manager
2) Attempt to login using the handle (this correctly raises an exception)
3) Create a handle to a fully function UCS manager
4) Attempt to login using the second handle (at this point, everything hangs indefinitely)

I've tried enabling threading mode on the handles, in hopes that the improved isolation would resolve the problem - sadly, it made no difference.

The [current version on PyPI](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/tldr), v0.3, has a bunch of bugs that have been fixed in the master version, such as #31 and #32.

I can make a [new release on github](https://github.com/tldr-pages/tldr-python-client/releases), but I can't update the PyPI package, as only @felixonmars has access. So this issue is two-fold:

1) make a new release here and on PyPI

2) add some people as owners/maintainers in PyPI ([example](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/mwclient#downloads))

@jcnelson [commented](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/issues/360) on new data PUT and GET endpoints:

> This has stabilized. Where's the best place to document it? The API table we have in the api/README.md file isn't expressive enough, since we need to also document how to construct the inputs and how to parse the outputs.

This opens up the general question that where should we document the API. Options are:

1) The [api_v1.md](https://github.com/blockstack/blockstack-core/blob/api/api/api/api_v1.md) file that is rendered by the node once it's deployed e.g., at https://core.blockstack.org. We can make that file more visible. It doesn't look very readable on Github though. 

2) api/README.md. 

The issue with using #2 is that #1 needs to be there regardless. So #2 will become a mirror and realistically out of sync with time.

Hey, I have a bug in baron's parsing, I took a quick look but am still new to Baron so I am unsure what fix would be needed.

Basically the get_space function of indentation_marker is checking to see if the node is less than length 3 *or* if the fourth item's length is equal to 0.

I have reproducible code that gives me an endl node that is length 3 (so it's not less than 3 length), and does not have a fourth item (because it's length 3).
Because of this I get an IndexError.

My first thought was that the code should be testing to see if the the node's length is less than 4 instead of 3.


If you ask Baron to parse the following code, you will see the issue:

```
def gfu():
    pass    
```

The whitespace after the pass statement is what causes it. 

It is syntactically valid code, just not pep8 compliant

I am using Baron 0.6.6 on python 2.7 Cent6 although this issue should appear on all from what I can tell.

issue_title: Is it possible for an agent to know the current model global timestep?

issue_title: Extraneous sshuttle.egg-info directory with wrong permissions on PyPI tarball
issue_body:
The title says it all: the [tarball provided on PyPI](https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/source/s/sshuttle/sshuttle-0.78.4.tar.gz) contains a  `sshuttle.egg-info` directory with wrong permissions (0600) on its files. 

It seems that this directory should be generated during the `setup.py build` instead of being present in the tarball, as it can break system-wide installations due to the wrong permissions on the egg files (making **every** python application unavailable for users without the ownership of these files, that usually is `root`).

issue_title: Missing Docs for cert.pem, chain.pem, fullchain.pem, etc
issue_body:
Searching the docs for `chain.pem` yields [no results](https://letsencrypt.readthedocs.org/en/latest/search.html?q=chain.pem&check_keywords=yes&area=default).

I just [asked on the mailing list](See https://groups.google.com/a/letsencrypt.org/forum/#!topic/client-dev/jE5uK4lPx5g) as well, but it seems it should be an issue on here as well.

Currently we have these files:

* privkey.pem
* cert.pem
* chain.pem
* fullchain.pem

But we don't know what goes in them. With the current dummy certificate it puts the **Root CA** in the `chain.pem` (which seems wrong) and `fullchain.pem` and that the **Server Certificate** goes into `cert.pem` and `fullchain.pem`, but we don't know where the **Intermediate Certificates** go.

This is extremely important because some webservers are lax, others are strict, others barf if you give too much info, others silently fail if you give to little.

I've already written some documentation and demo code based on assumption, but I already know that that's going to blow up in my face if I don't get the details correct.

In order for greatest compatibility with various servers it would seem that we need a file that bundles the server + intermediate certificates, and *excludes* the root certificate. I'm hoping that's what `cert.pem` is intended to be.

Once I know the technical details as to what these files are supposed to represent, I may open another issue to discuss the need for additional files.

issue_title: Challenge files should get chowned like acme-challenge folder
issue_body:
Currently when you use the webroot plugin, it tries to chown the ache-challenge folder to the parent permissions (e.g. `$user:nobody`) - which is awesome, the thing is the challenge files are still owned by root, meaning servers that runs with symlink protection (basically disallowing users from reading other users files) it results in not being able to validate the challenge because the challenge files are owned by root.

What would make sense is to either allow setting the uid/gid of which the challenge files should be owned  (might be I want the files to be owned as `www-data:www-data`) - or even better even just copy the block as here https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt/plugins/webroot.py#L73 - put it in the `_perform_single` function to do the same for the `validation_file`.

This would be a big plus, specially for hosting providers, currently we have to start a sub-process that chowns everything in the challenge folder to make letsencrypt work on a shared hosting environment that does prevent reading files from other users.

I'm getting the following error: 
```
Failed authorization procedure. pes.huperwebs.nl (http-01): urn:acme:error:unauthorized 
:: The client lacks sufficient authorization

```

This is the `pes_huperwebs_nl.ini` file:
```ini

----

-----

## Debug process 

I'm sending some kind of token to the server, which is of the format `token.format`. The dot is there, therefore it parses the 'parts' successfully, but the `token` part is apparently not well formed (i.e.: doesn't match regex `^[\\w-]{43}$`. 

After modifying the source a bit, and looking at the `letsencrypt/boulder` source, I found out this was caused by malformed response by `nginx` (bad configuration). I had to manually create a `location` entry in the nginx config file, because all other requests are being forwarded (reverse proxy) to a Go webserver (which isn't suited for this challenge-response).

```

This doesn't happen with a single domain. Poking around, it looks like multiple snakeoil keys are created but only one cert, which is probably the reason for the mismatch.

I guess I'm not the first one to raise the issue, but I haven't found anything yet by searching.

I'm trying to automate VM deployment with a letsencrypt certificate. So I have tried to remove user interaction as much as possible and reached:

```
sudo /opt/letsencrypt/letsencrypt-auto certonly --manual -m contact@example.com -d example.com -t --manual-public-ip-logging-ok --agree-tos
```

But if I add the `-n` option, I get:

> `PluginError('Running manual mode non-interactively is not supported',)`

I'm guessing the point is that manual mode *does* require user interaction hence cannot be fully done in non-interactive mode. At least during the file-validation step.

Which is why I'd like to discuss splitting the command in two steps:

```
sudo /opt/letsencrypt/letsencrypt-auto certonly --manual -m contact@example.com -d example.com -t --manual-public-ip-logging-ok --agree-tos --generate-file /home/user
```
which would create the file `/home/user/.well-known/acme-challenge/buCgmexKDQypJfWE4-XmUfP1cpILzsBR4UXRE_o6iuc` with the expected content and then running

```
sudo /opt/letsencrypt/letsencrypt-auto certonly --manual -m contact@example.com -d example.com -t --manual-public-ip-logging-ok --agree-tos --validate
```
would finish the process.

Would this make sense? Did I miss something?

issue_title: Feature request: set secondaryFiles in workflow step input
issue_body:
I would like to be able to set `secondaryFiles` for inputs of workflows steps. For example, GATK requires the following reference genome files: FASTA, FAI and DICT. The last two files are generated by different programs. To make a workflow fully reproducible, I think the same workflow should create those files before running the step that requires all files related with reference genome.

## Expected Behavior
To be able to set `secondaryFiles` for inputs of workflow steps.

## Actual Behavior
Related files should be set in job file or pass them using additional params.

## Workflow Code
``` yml
steps:
  create_sequence_dictionary:
    run: /home/welliton/git/tool-picard/picard-createsequencedictionary.cwl
    in:
      reference: ref
    out: [output_file]
  faidx:
    run: /home/welliton/git/tool-samtools/samtools-faidx.cwl
    in:
      ref_fasta: ref
    out: [index_file]

# Align FASTQ files, build BAM index ...

  base_recalibrator:
    run: /home/welliton/git/tool-gatk/gatk-baserecalibrator.cwl
    scatter: [input_file]
    in:
      input_file: bam_index/output
      reference_sequence: ref
# faidx/index_file and create_sequence_dictionary/output_file should be 
# secondaryFiles of reference_sequence
      reference_index: faidx/index_file
      reference_dictionary: create_sequence_dictionary/output_file
      intervals: intervals
      knownSites: knownSites
      out:
        valueFrom: $(inputs.input_file.basename).recal_data.table
    out: [output]
```

Input example:

``` yml
ref:
  class: File
  path: Homo_sapiens_assembly38.fasta
intervals:
  class: File
  path: targets_hg38.interval_list
knownSites:
  - class: File
    path: dbsnp_144.hg38.vcf.gz
    secondaryFiles:
      - class: File
        path: dbsnp_144.hg38.vcf.gz.tbi
  - class: File
    path: Mills_and_1000G_gold_standard.indels.hg38.vcf.gz
    secondaryFiles:
      - class: File
        path: Mills_and_1000G_gold_standard.indels.hg38.vcf.gz.tbi
```

Hi.

Assuming a model uses custom Marshmallow fields, those do not appear in `FIELD_MAPPING` and therefore are not documented properly: default (type, format) is ('string', None).

If `_get_json_type_for_field` used `isinstance` rather than `type`, fields inheriting from Marshmallow fields would at least be treated like their parent, but this wouldn't be totally satisfying.

OpenAPI spec allows custom allows custom formats for fields, like apispec does `Email`, for instance, 

    marshmallow.fields.Email: ('string', 'email'),

Is there a way to pass custom field mappings to apispec?

If not, would this be considered a relevant feature request?

A list of custom types ((`CUSTOM_FIELD_MAPPING`) could be appended to `FIELD_MAPPING`. Or better, `CUSTOM_FIELD_MAPPING` would be checked first, so as to allow overriding existing mappings in `FIELD_MAPPING`.

It'd be pretty nice to have versions associated with objects and have marshmallow be able to handle the request for an older version; similar to what this code is doing @ https://github.com/openstack/oslo.versionedobjects/blob/master/oslo_versionedobjects/base.py#L452 (that code is openstack specific, and it seems quite similar to marshmallow in general); 

Maybe there is room for this kind of feature in marshmallow?

I'm talking about [werkzeug.datastructures.ImmutableMultiDict](http://werkzeug.pocoo.org/docs/0.9/datastructures/#werkzeug.datastructures.ImmutableMultiDict).  This is the type flask.request.values will give you. (actually it's a CombinedMultiDict but they have the same interface).

You can get a single value out of it with ```[]``` or ```get()```, or a list of values with ```getlist()```.  It would be neat if marshmallow's ```fields.List()``` knew to check for the ```getlist()``` method, in case it is given a multidict that can return a list in this way.

issue_title: Function attributes differ after pickling with presence of decorators
issue_body:
Example:
```python3
import functools                                                                                                                                                                                      
import cloudpickle

def identity_decorator(func):
    @functools.wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        return func(*args, **kwargs)
    return wrapper

@identity_decorator
def identity(x):
    return x

identity_pkl = cloudpickle.dumps(identity)
unpickled_identity = cloudpickle.loads(identity_pkl)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    print('identity.__name__ =', identity.__name__)
    print('unpickled_identity.__name__ =', unpickled_identity.__name__)
```

Actual result:
```
identity.__name__ = identity                                                                                                                                                                          
unpickled_identity.__name__ = wrapper
```

```
identity.__name__ = identity                                                                                                                                                                          
unpickled_identity.__name__ = identity
```

This has been problematic in my application which uses the function name to determine the root of a caching key (which then ends up being the same for all functions with a decorator applied to them!) I note dill seems to handle this case, so I believe it should be possible to add support to cloudpickle (although I'm not sure how easy a change it is).

Looking at the source for the precision at k implementation, it looks like it divides the sum of the true positives by k, which is fine. However, as the wikipedia article states, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Evaluation_measures_(information_retrieval)#Precision_at_K ,  one shortcoming of this is that a perfect recommender can still get a low score if the number of test interactions is lower than k. So one implementation I've seen to remedy this is to take min `min(k, num_test_items)` so that it still works in the traditional sense, but also is adaptive for small test sets. What do you think?

issue_title: irradiance.disc has unbounded clearness index calculation
issue_body:
In `pvlib.irradiance.disc` the clearness index `kt` is calculated as `kt = ghi / I0h`. Negative values of `kt` are removed but large values are not.  In rare circumstances, `ghi` (e.g., from a clear sky model) greatly exceeds `I0h` yielding `kt` values upwards of 80.  These values lead to a numpy overflow warning at

`delta_kn` = a + b + np.exp(c*am)`

because `c` is very large.

**To demonstrate:**
`import pvlib`
`from pandas import Timestamp`
` ts = Timestamp('2016-07-19 06:11:00-0600', tz='US/Mountain', freq='T')`
`pvlib.irradiance.disc(ghi=1.0, zenith=89.99, datetime_or_doy=ts)`

**Expected behavior**
`irradiance.disc` should use the new `clearness_index` function which sets an upper bound on `kt`

**Versions:**
 - ``pvlib.__version__``: 0.5.1
The DIRINDEX model is a variation of the DIRINT model for decomposition that considers the clear sky index. Reference Daryl Myers Solar Radiation CRC press, chapter 5.

issue_title: Unify bptt_batchify interface between LanguageModelStream and LanguageModelDataset
issue_body:
Currently there are at least the following discrepancies:

- `LanguageModelStream.bptt_batchify()` returns a mask
  - The mask can be recovered by `mask = data != vocab[vocab.padding_token]`
  - We can either change the `LanguageModelDataset.bptt_batchify()` interface to also return a mask or use above formula to recover there mask where neede.
  - I vote for above formula, as it explicitly defines the mask and may be clearer to users. Users anyways need a line `mask = mask.as_in_context(ctx)` which could then just be replaced by `mask = data != vocab[vocab.padding_token]`. However, I'd also be ok with chaning the `LanguageModelDataset.bptt_batchify()`.
  - `LanguageModelStream.bptt_batchify()` is smart and never includes `<eos>` in the data batch but only in the target  batch.
  - `LanguageModelDataset.bptt_batchify()` instead assumes the data doesn't contain `<bos>` but only `eos`.
  - See https://github.com/dmlc/gluon-nlp/blob/master/gluonnlp/data/stream.py#L415

Commit a0961b1cf451255ea3372787d6ee4c78cee700a8 modified lasagne/layers/pool.py
method ```pool_output_length``` to include a stride argument, but ```FeaturePoolLayer``` makes a call to this method without this argument, raising an exception.

More details:

```class FeaturePoolLayer(Layer)```
defines the method
```
def get_output_shape_for(self, input_shape):
        output_shape = list(input_shape)  # make a mutable copy
        output_shape[self.axis] = pool_output_length(input_shape[self.axis],
                                                     self.ds)
        return tuple(output_shape)
```

which makes a call to pool_output_length, but is missing the stride argument
```def pool_output_length(input_length, ds, st, ignore_border=True, pad=0):```

Should stride be set to ds if None like with ```MaxPool2DLayer```?

Right now, in my model I am using the ElemwiseMergeLayer a lot. Especially since residual networks and skip layers, this has become a very popular approach (compared to when lasagne started).

Could we override the __add__, __radd__, __mul__ and __rmul__ on the Layer class, to create a suitable ElemwiseMergeLayer? This will clean the code for some models considerably. And it would be more intuitive, I reckon.

I have been thinking for a while, but I don't see what would be an alternative operation performed by the operator in this context. I think it is kind of straightforward.

I would implement it myself, and would provide support for adding (and multiplying) constants and numpy arrays. If there is support for this feature from the main developers, of course.

fs provides a great abstraction and useful operations by multiple types of FS objects, a common usage of filesystem is performing many tasks implemented in different functions on one given FS object, like:

```
def high_level_task(directory_path):
    with OSFS(directory_path) as fs:
        low_level_task0(some_args, fs)
        with fs.opendir(sub_dir_path) as sub_fs:
            low_level_task1(some_args, sub_fs)
        low_level_task1(some_args, fs)

def low_level_task0(some_args, fs):
    ... (operations on fs)


def low_level_task1(some_args, fs):
    ... (operations on fs)
```

FS object need to be passed everywhere. But using `with` statement, it should not be a necessary requirement. An example is `variable_scope` in TensorFlow.

Thus if we has an function `get_filesystem()` to get current FS constructed by `with` statement, we can make previous code cleaner like:

```
def high_level_task(directory_path):
    with OSFS(directory_path) as fs:
        low_level_task0(some_args)
        with fs.opendir(sub_dir_path):
            low_level_task1(some_args)
        low_level_task1(some_args)

def low_level_task0(some_args):
    with fs.get_filesystem() as fs:
        ... (operations on fs)

def low_level_task1(some_args):
    with fs.get_filesystem() as fs:
        ... (operations on fs)
```

Further more following functionality may be useful:
 `get_filesystem()` returns default file system when no file system is constructed 
 `get_filesystem(name:str)` returns some other FS object by its name

issue_title: Non-unicode log messages can break on sender name
issue_body:
Hello! I work at edx.org, where we make use of this library to send bulk emails from educators to learners.

Recently, [we ran into an issue with some Spanish courses](https://openedx.atlassian.net/browse/EDUCATOR-1773). Specifically, [this logging statement](https://github.com/django-ses/django-ses/blob/v0.8.3.1/django_ses/__init__.py#L217-L222) causes a `UnicodeEncodeError` when `message.from_email` contains non-ascii characters. In our case, the `from_email` value is constructed as `{course_title} Course Staff <{some_valid_noreply_addr}>`, and for these courses, the titles contain accented vowels.

It's worth noting that the emails are sent without any issue, the only problem seems to be the logging statements as far as I can tell.

Here is a fairly minimal recreation taken from a pdb session I just ran:

```
(Pdb) from_addr
u'"\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3\xf3" Course Staff <asdasd-no-reply@example.com>'
(Pdb) print "send_messages.sent from='{}'".format(from_addr)
*** UnicodeEncodeError: 'ascii' codec can't encode characters in position 1-15: ordinal not in range(128)
(Pdb) print u"send_messages.sent from='{}'".format(from_addr)
send_messages.sent from='"├│├│├│├│├│├│├│├│├│├│├│├│├│├│├│" Course Staff <asdasd-no-reply@example.com>'
```

Would there be any drawback to making all log messages unicode by default? I'm happy to put together a PR if you'd like.

Thanks, and have a great weekend!

issue_title: Wrong category axis label for barh

issue_body:
`get_field_by_name` should return field instance as first argument.

It is True
```python
InheritorModel._meta.get_field_by_name('money_currency')[0].__class__.__name__ == 'CurrencyField'
```

But the same code returns different result if this was called before:
```python
InheritorModel._meta.get_field_by_name('money')
```

It works in a reverse way. If `get_field_by_name` was called with `money_currency` before, then for `money` `CurrencyField` will be returned instead of `MoneyField`.

This bug is related with caching in Django 1.8+

issue_title: Is it possible to get rid of implict float conversions in django-money?
issue_body:
Hi,

the current implementation appears to implicitly convert certain data types to float [at](https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/b4b4e09e9f33dab6615cd0dc92161fbd2f6de004/djmoney/models/fields.py#L65) [some](https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/b4b4e09e9f33dab6615cd0dc92161fbd2f6de004/djmoney/models/fields.py#L257) [point](https://github.com/django-money/django-money/blob/b4b4e09e9f33dab6615cd0dc92161fbd2f6de004/djmoney/templatetags/djmoney.py#L81). 

Considering the [instability](http://0.30000000000000004.com/) of a computer's FPU, it would be nice to get rid of these occurrences and use Decimal instead where practicable in order to circumvent inaccurate calculations with monetary numbers because that would be *really* bad.

issue_title: requirements.txt has pymavlink pinned to 2.0.6 instead of 2.0.8
issue_body:
pymavlink 2.0.8 opens up the door to working on Python3.

This is part of PR https://github.com/dronekit/dronekit-python/pull/673 but could also be done separately.

RemovedInDjango19Warning: Model class django.contrib.contenttypes.models.ContentType 
    doesn't declare an explicit app_label and either isn't in an application in INSTALLED_APPS 
    or else was imported before its application was loaded. This will no longer be supported in 
    Django 1.9.

I've pushed a quick fix for now that is safe but a touch dirty. I've just moved the imports inside their respective methods.

A proper fix would be to not import models in our __init__.py files as Django 1.8+ is stricter about the order of imports and we end up importing ContentTypes too early. I'll add a bit more detail here when I have time.

| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |

| Firmware Version                   | 05.08.14.00 | 
| Operating System & Version |   Win 10  | 
| Platform                                 | PC (C#.NET)  |
| SDK Version                            |  2.9.1 | 

Hi,

I have a device which has a D410 depth module with no RealSense colour camera, but it does also have a rear-facing webcam. I want to be able to map from the colour photo from the platform camera (webcam) to the depth data so I can tap on the colour image to get the corresponding depth value. The physical distance between the right lens of the depth camera and the colour camera is approx. (93mm, 17mm, 10mm), and they have different focal lengths, fields of view etc. I am using C# to further complicate the matter.

I've gotten the extrinsic calibration between the two cameras, as well as their individual intrinsics, using OpenCv; and I've gotten them both streaming at the same time (using `Sensor.Start` for the platform camera and `Pipeline.Start` for depth) but I'm struggling with doing the actual mapping.

I have tried several approaches: reverse engineering the `Align` class & implementing` align_images` myself in C# (wasn't mapping correctly & was extremely slow); exposing a new entry point into the existing `Align` class allowing me to inject my own extrinsics (`align.Process` always times out); and finally, using the new `SoftwareDevice` interface, which I've had a go at wrapping myself and seems fine up until I pass my `Syncer` object to `Sensor.Start` where I get a compile error that `Syncer` can't convert to `FrameQueue` or `FrameCallback`.

So anyway, my real question is: do any of these approaches sound reasonable? In your opinion, do you think what I want to do is possible with the current SDK (or with relatively small modifications to it)? 
It's entirely likely that I've made mistakes while modifying the SDK as I'm not very well versed in C++ or interop stuff, but I don't know if it's worth me spending the time going into it if it turns out that either I'll need to do loads of extra stuff beyond just getting the wrapper working, or you guys are working on something that'll make it really easy, or the differences between the cameras makes it a really hard problem to solve without lots of expertise (that I lack!)

Thanks for any help or advice you may be able to offer.
James

issue_title: join of data from different data sources in graphene query
issue_body:
I have two data sources , one is `PostgreSQL table` and the other is `CSV file`. I used `SQLAlchemy-graphene` to create the model for postgreSQL and I used `graphene.objectType `for creating the class Model of csvfile.

Now I want to join data from these models. I dont know how can i define the join query. Please guide me. Thanks.

here is my models and schema:


```
class Sheetheight(graphene.ObjectType):
    ullid= graphene.Float()
    id=graphene.ID()
    sheetid=graphene.String()
    passcolum=graphene.Int()

   
class Dzlog(Base):
    ullid=Column(Float)
    id=Column(Integer,primary_key=True)
    date=Column(Date)
    sheetid=Column(Integer)
    passcolumn=Column(Integer)
    color=Column(String)
    headid=Column(Integer)

```

I am able to query each singles classes separately. 

This is how I resolve the query for postgreSQL table:
```

issue_title: Commissioning: Configure and use respective PSKd for each Joiner
issue_body:
To pass certification test 8.2.2(Joiner Router), we need a way to configure respective PSKd for each Joiner, and Commissioner should use the corresponding PSKd for DTLS handshake with the Joiner.

8.2.2(Joiner Router), test steps:

Step 1. First golden device:
- form network and start commissioner
- configure DUT's PSKd, as well as its EUI-64;
- configure Second Golden device's PSKd, as well as its EUI-64;

Step 2. DUT:
 - start commissioning process
 - attach network, and become Joiner Router

Step 3. Second golden device:
 - add first golden device(commissioner)'s mac address to black list
 - start commissioning

So we need to:

- Add a CLI to configure Joiner's info: EUI-64 and PSKd: `commission add eui-64 pskd`;
- Maintain a Joiner list, including their EUI-64 and PSKd;
- Use corresponding PSKd for DTLS handshake with each Joiner.

issue_title: agate-sql: VARCHAR used when TEXT or BLOB should be for large fields?
issue_body:
This may be an issue with an underlying library, but I thought I'd start here.

When generating a MySQL schema from a CSV file with:

`csvsql --dialect mysql --snifflimit 100000 my.csv --tables my_table`

I get a result that includes something like 

```
my_big_content_field VARCHAR(41062),
my_other_big_content_field VARCHAR(23600),
```

and of course if I try to load that CREATE statement in to MySQL, it complains:

`ERROR 1074 (42000) at line 1: Column length too big for column 'my_big_content_field' (max = 21845); use BLOB or TEXT instead`

I don't see any obvious way to force csvsql to switch to using a TEXT column type, so I'm left with manually updating the generated schema before trying to use it. Am I missing something?

issue_title: Do not convert [cr][lf] end of line to [lf]
issue_body:
RFC 4180 is not really a standard and explicitly "documents the format that seems to be followed by most implementations". But many implementations strictly follow RFC 4180. And it [says that](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc4180#page-2):
> 1.  Each record is located on a separate line, delimited by a line break (CRLF).

But every tools of csvkit seem to convert [cr][lf] end of line to [lf] (at least under Linux). It think it shouldn't. Every other conversions of csvkit tools follow RFC 4180 but not the end of line. For example, CSVLint is yelling when we have [lf] end of lines:
> Structural problem: Non-standard Line Breaks on row 1
> 
> Your CSV appears to use LF line-breaks. While this will be fine in most cases, RFC 4180 specifies that CSV files should use CR-LF (a carriage-return and line-feed pair, e.g. \r\n). This may be labelled as "Windows line endings" on some systems.

issue_title: Does Dask-ML support Custom Transformers to Use in Pipeline
issue_body:
Thanks for this awesome project!

I have a scikit-learn pipeline combining some custom transformers for feature-engineering with a classifier at the end (xgboost). Does Dask-ML accept user-defined pipeline step/classes like Sklearn does? If so, what are the requirements (e.g. "implement fit, transform, and return a Dask Dataframe?") And are there any classes a Dask-ML pipeline step should inherit from (e.g. in sklearn all my custom transforms inherit from `BaseEstimator` in order to get `get_params`... see: https://stackoverflow.com/a/39093021/1870832)

The Dask-ML docs are pretty great in general but I couldn't find an answer or example on this. Sorry if I'm missing it somewhere.

Most dask packages follow the following naming convention:

1.  dask-foo for package name on PyPI, and github
2.  dask_foo for import name in Python

The `dask-ml` package is currently imported as `daskml` rather than `dask_ml`.  The underscore is a bit of a pain, so I can see how the current name would be preferred.  Still though, I thought I'd bring this up.  We may want to switch for conformity's sake.

cc @TomAugspurger @jcrist

issue_title: Cmd2.redirector isn't honored any more, either make it work or deprecate it
issue_body: With the merge of PR #370, `Cmd2.redirector` is no longer honored: the redirector symbol is currently hard-coded as '>'. The documentation still states that you can set `Cmd2.redirector` to something like '->' and have the parsing logic honor that change. We need to either fix the code to match the documentation, or deprecate the attribute and fix the documentation to say that you can't change the redirector.

issue_body: Remove the usage of ``pexpect`` by unit tests and the one unit test which uses.  This test is always skipped on the continuous integration (CI) platforms since it doesn't work there, so it isn't doing us any good.

I'm not entirely sure how EasyEnsemble should be used, but I feel like it might be easier if it was a meta-estimator.
Let's say I want to implement a random forest using EasyEnsemble as an estimator. I have no idea how to do that easily. If it was a meta-estimator, I could just do ``EasyEnsemble(DecisionTreeClassifier(max_features="auto"))``, which would be nice. Ideally this would set the random seeds, too.

I ended up with this solution, which works, but is verbose and probably inefficient:
```python
from sklearn.ensemble import VotingClassifier
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier
def make_resample_tree(random_state=0):
    tree = make_imb_pipeline(RandomUnderSampler(random_state=random_state, replacement=True),
                             DecisionTreeClassifier(max_features='auto', random_state=random_state))
    return "tree_i".format(random_state), tree
classifiers = [make_resample_tree(i) for i in range(100)]
resampled_rf = VotingClassifier(classifiers, voting='soft')
```

issue_title: Support for similar to boost::python::indexing _suite functionality
issue_body:
Hello, Wenzel!
Currently, I'm using boost::python to export C++ types to Python. However, I'm planning to move to pybind11 as it seems to be 'lighter' than boost::python, looks elegant and has support for numpy.
However, I have quite a lot of STL(`std::vector, std::map, std::unordered_map`) types exported; and I'm using indexing_suite utilities for doing that. Its major advantage is that it reduces the burden of explicitly defining general container methods such as e.g. `append` (for `std::vector`), etc. The range of such methods is the same for each and every container object of specific type and, in most cases, referring to such objects from python should be analogous to using python dicts and lists. 

Are there any plans to add such functionality?
Would be a PR valuable?

issue_title: Simplify http instructions in manual plugin
issue_body:
Right now when you run the manual plugin for an HTTP challenge, you get the following message:

```
Make sure your web server displays the following content at
http://4544ad.com/.well-known/acme-challenge/UlK01OTOxzjsZO74kSzj53goMdIaJKlf6xEVoAlImBE before continuing:

UlK01OTOxzjsZO74kSzj53goMdIaJKlf6xEVoAlImBE.W1iiTykicy7OqjL-Fu3XxEut3RTonSfTtbV6mRA2u4g

If you don't have HTTP server configured, you can run the following
command on the target server (as root):

mkdir -p /tmp/certbot/public_html/.well-known/acme-challenge
cd /tmp/certbot/public_html
printf "%s" UlK01OTOxzjsZO74kSzj53goMdIaJKlf6xEVoAlImBE.W1iiTykicy7OqjL-Fu3XxEut3RTonSfTtbV6mRA2u4g > .well-known/acme-challenge/UlK01OTOxzjsZO74kSzj53goMdIaJKlf6xEVoAlImBE
# run only once per server:
$(command -v python2 || command -v python2.7 || command -v python2.6) -c \
"import BaseHTTPServer, SimpleHTTPServer; \
s = BaseHTTPServer.HTTPServer(('', 5002), SimpleHTTPServer.SimpleHTTPRequestHandler); \
s.serve_forever()" 
```

I would argue that the code at the bottom is visual noise and distracts from the more important information up top. I also think that most people using the manual plugin with the HTTP challenge don't want these instructions to set up a Python server, because they are probably planning to push the challenge file to an already-running web server of some sort.

I think we should rip out everything below "If you don't have...". Note that the integration tests, last I checked, expect to parse out this message and run it, so that integration test will need to be updated.

issue_title: Discuss: Freezing + PIP as distribution model
issue_body:
It seems like Certbot encounters numerous issues around distribution and dependency management.

It seems like [freezing](https://python-guide-pt-br.readthedocs.io/en/latest/shipping/freezing/#freezing-your-code-ref) using a tool like [cx_Freeze](https://anthony-tuininga.github.io/cx_Freeze/) or [PyInstaller](http://www.pyinstaller.org/) would greatly simplify the experience for end-users: a release would be a sets of binaries, including all necessary dependencies, for each platform. The main drawback of this is that it means that new Certbot releases would be required to pick up fixes (including security fixes) in dependencies or Python itself, but it comes with a significant advantage that everyone using a given version of Certbot will be using a single well-known and tested version of each dependency.

Essentially: ship an end-user application instead of a collection of python modules.

Those who use Certbot as a library (and more savvy users who want control over the versions of dependencies used) could get Certbot via PIP.

<!-- Provide a general summary of the issue in the Title above -->
Internal server error caused on search as "pt_analyzer" is missing

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/9539326/36855172-b9a53ffe-1d6a-11e8-9697-156ed61bc928.png)

## Detailed Description
uData is loooking for a specific Elasticsearch analyser depending on "DEFAULT_LANGUAGE" setting.
https://github.com/opendatateam/udata/blob/2c3885029cbf9401010b2832ca2999afaf6f342c/udata/search/__init__.py#L113-L115

In our case as our default language setting is "pt" this custom analyser doesn't exist, causing an AttributeError:
https://github.com/opendatateam/udata/blob/2c3885029cbf9401010b2832ca2999afaf6f342c/udata/search/analysis.py#L71 

## Possible Implementation
<!-- Not obligatory, but suggest an idea for implementing addition or change -->
In our case we will need also a custom analyser named "pt_analyzer" because we will need special Elasticsearch filters as for example the "ASCII Folding Token filter" or "asciifolding", but it would be nice to set a default analyser when the i18n custom is missing.
https://github.com/opendatateam/udata/blob/2c3885029cbf9401010b2832ca2999afaf6f342c/udata/search/__init__.py#L113-L115

by changing line L115 to:
 `return getattr(analysis, '{0}_analyzer'.format(language)) or getattr(analysis, 'standard')`

Let me know if this change makes sense, and also if we could add a custom "pt_analyzer". 
Can we open a pull request? :)

Best!

issue_title: Implement API endpoint for querying connection to token network
issue_body:
## Problem Definition

We have API endpoints to [connect](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/f0d57190aa92258e7e4cf70b6c845719d6bb2121/docs/rest_api.rst#connecting-to-a-token-network) and to [leave](https://github.com/raiden-network/raiden/blob/f0d57190aa92258e7e4cf70b6c845719d6bb2121/docs/rest_api.rst#leaving-a-token-network) a token network.

Once a user is connected to a token network, a connection manager has been created for that network which manages the user's connections to channels of that network. The problem is that there is no way for the user to tell if such a connection manager has been created once it is there.

## Solution

Create a way for users to query if they are connected (in other words if a ConnectionManager is active) to a token network through the REST API.

The simplest way to achieve this is to add the possibility to perform a `GET` request to the connection endpoint for a token. e.g.:

`GET /api/<version>/connection/<token_address>`

If there is no connection it should return a `204 No content` and if there is a connection a `200 OK` with the allocated funds for the connection manager, much like the `PUT` request does.

issue_title: Rethink safe_to_wait in the context of the SecretRegistry
issue_body:
Rethink how much should a node wait before sending the secret reveal on-chain transaction.
With the old approach, we would need to take into account the `closeChannel` transaction + all the `unlock` transactions.

issue_title: Offer ability to force google account chooser.
issue_body:
It's possible to force google to display the account chooser screen when the user is redirected to the `auth` endpoint by including a `prompt` query parameter. [This SO post](http://stackoverflow.com/a/14393492/7322725) goes into a bit more detail. 

My app has users logging in and out with different Google accounts, and the default google auth endpoint seems to remember the last account they'd authed with, and sharply bypasses the account chooser. 

I'm able to get the desired behaviour by adding `params['prompt'] = 'select_account'` to `social_core.backends.oauth.BaseOAuth2.auth_params` :

```py
    def auth_params(self, state=None):
        client_id, client_secret = self.get_key_and_secret()
        params = {
            'client_id': client_id,
            'redirect_uri': self.get_redirect_uri(state)
        }
        if self.STATE_PARAMETER and state:
            params['state'] = state
        if self.RESPONSE_TYPE:
            params['response_type'] = self.RESPONSE_TYPE
        
        params['prompt'] = 'select_account' ## <----  Adding in forced account chooser
        return params
```

I'm not exactly sure the best way to make this configurable, being the many levels of abstraction in this lib. Think you could point me in the right direction and I'll submit a PR @omab ?

issue_title: "Task is a driver task and so the object created by ray.put could not be reconstructed."
issue_body:
<!--
General questions should be asked on the mailing list ray-dev@googlegroups.com.

Before submitting an issue, please fill out the following form.
-->

### System information
- **OS Platform and Distribution (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)**: Mac OS Sierra 10.13.3
- **Ray installed from (source or binary)**: pip install ray
- **Ray version**: 0.4.0
- **Python version**: 3.6.5
- **Exact command to reproduce**: just python [source.py]

<!--
You can obtain the Ray version with

python -c "import ray; print(ray.__version__)"
-->

### Describe the problem
When continuously putting and getting large objects result in error saying:
  
"The task with ID 7e6d77402f393fac87ca0da4818f1e62c7c8edad is a driver task and so the object created by ray.put could not be reconstructed."

"You can inspect errors by running ray.error_info()", which actually == []. 

### Source code / logs
```
import ray
import time
import numpy as np
SIZE_MB = [0.001 * pow(1.23, i) for i in range(55, 60)] # Towards 201 MB object.
total_nums = [1,2,4,8,16]

def test_get_throughput():
	"""
	Test single-threaded maximum get throughput achievable by Ray
	We measure the actual throughput by first call get
	Then copy the result of get onto a numpy array
	so that we can force memory copying...
	"""

	def do_measure_throughput(size, reuse_rate, num, rand_ids, target_array):
		for i in range(num):
			target_array += ray.get(rand_ids[i])

	for size in SIZE_MB:
		rand_ids = [ray.put(np.random.rand(int(size * 250 * 1000))) for _ in range(total_nums[-1])]
		for num in total_nums:
			my_tag = num
			target_array = np.zeros(int(size * 250 * 1000))
			print(num, size)
			do_measure_throughput(size, reuse_rate, num, rand_ids, target_array)

if __name__ == "__main__":
	ray.init()
	print("Testing throughput for get...")
	test_get_throughput()


```

issue_title: Kill actor workers when the actor handle goes out of scope.
issue_body:
Whenever we create a new actor, a new worker process is created. That worker process will be killed when the overall script exits. This can cause problems for workloads that create actors within a loop, for example

```python
import ray

ray.init()

@ray.remote
class Foo(object):
    def __init__(self):
        pass

while True:
  f = Foo.remote()
```

In this example, the script eventually fails (presumably because of the problem described in 476).

However, we could begin removing these actor workers much earlier. Once we no longer have access to the handle and once we've finished executing all actor method calls, then we no longer need the worker.

Some thoughts:

1. This could be implemented essentially by submitting a task in the actor handle's destructor which kills the worker when it executes.
2. This may need to be modified if we start allowing actor handles to be passed around to different tasks (because we'll need to make sure that the actor handle has gone out of scope everywhere before killing the worker).

issue_title: [tune] Implement population based training as a ray.tune TrialScheduler
issue_body:
We should implement https://deepmind.com/blog/population-based-training-neural-networks/ as a custom TrialScheduler in ray.tune.

PBT implements hyperparameter perturbations within individual algorithm runs, which can substantially improve efficiency compared to current schedulers that fix parameters starting from the beginning of the run (e.g. MedianStoppingRule, HyperBand). In addition, this automatically discovers reasonable hyperparam schedules, which is something completely not possible with current schedulers.

The implementation could look something like this:
- We use the ray.tune json spec as the initial model population
- The trial scheduler periodically perturbs promising trials / removes poor trials as described in the paper: https://arxiv.org/abs/1711.09846

This would be the first trial scheduler that creates new trials during runs, so there might be some kinks to work out with how results are logged. In addition, we might need to provide some way to annotate params that should be perturbed.

Right now the OpenAPI 2.0 spec is dubious on how to handle this, but several services like `codegen` allow defining open schemas by setting `additionalProperties: {}`. I guess it should be added, specially if the colander mapping is set to type `colander.Mapping(unknown='preserve')`.

Original discussion on swagger-codegen: https://github.com/swagger-api/swagger-codegen/issues/1318#issuecomment-146302879

issue_title: Can not play outputted files in VLC when saving from YouTube
issue_body: When doing `streamlink youtube-url best -o ./file` the resulting file does not play in VLC. It plays in MPC on Windows with CCCP installed and it plays in mplayer on Linux. For whatever reason VLC can not play the file. I'm not sure if it's specific to VLC or ffmpeg, but I was able to load the file into Blender without issue.

issue_title: TF model pruning APIs and tensorpack
issue_body:
Issue: Usage Questions

1. What you did:
I am trying to use TF model pruning API through tensorpack. For this, I did the following:
(a) modified tensorpack's conv2d function to include the weight pruning "apply_mask" functionality. For this, I simply added a "masking" variable which is set if I want to turn on the pruning functionality. See line 120-165 (line 157 is where this is done). File attached.
(b) Included the other pruning APIs in the trainer side of code as tensorpack callbacks -- see PruningCallback in sparse-cifar-convnet.py. It is a mod of cifar-convnet.py is examples/basics.

2. What you observed, e.g. the entire log:
Log file is attached. The problem is that the sparsity summaries report 0 sparsity which says that pruning is not taking place.

```
[0607 14:37:29 @monitor.py:433] PruningCallback/cifar10_pruning_summaries/conv1.1//mask_0/sparsity: 0

issue_title: Provide a way to automatically treat all async tests as trio tests
issue_body:
We don't want to do this by default, because then we get into the situation where all the different pytest-{asyncio,trio,curio,...} plugins fight over which tests are theirs. And we want to preserve the option to mark individual tests with `@pytest.mark.trio`, for projects that want to use multiple pytest-* plugins at the same time.

But for the common case of a project that uses only trio, there should be some way to opt-in to automatic detection of async tests as trio tests.

An option in `pytest.ini` is one obvious thing. We should also give a way for `conftest.py` to opt-in directly, for the use of projects that ship their tests along with their source code but can't easily ship a `pytest.ini`.

What would be really neat was if we could limit the `conftest.py` opt-in to the subdirectory that `conftest.py` applies to. I'm not sure if there's a way to do that.
should probably figure out why and fix it at some point

I think it is time for us to discuss the creation of contributing guidelines. Benefit would be that it helps others who want to contribute to this project but also as a documentation for the rest of us. I don't think we need to go all out on this like [pandas](http://pandas.pydata.org/pandas-docs/stable/contributing.html) or [numpy](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy-dev/dev/index.html) but rather keep it relatively short, like [rasterio](https://github.com/mapbox/rasterio/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.rst).

What are things we should include? I'll start with:

- [x] design principles / code style guide
- [x] developing environment / How to create a PR for a fix or feature?
- [x] testing / writing tests (Travis and local)
- [x] documentation and changelog
- [x] release and packaging
